intent_questions.question: What behavior must be proven?
intent_questions.self_answer: GET / from the README Deno server should return HTTP success and the exact body Hello, world!.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may test CLI internals, another route, or a generated server not present in README evidence.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Replace the route and body only with exact public repository evidence.

intent_questions.question: What is the system boundary: Runner target, Mocker dependency, hook host, or configuration-as-code?
intent_questions.self_answer: The Runner target is an externally-started Deno HTTP process on 127.0.0.1:8000; QaaS YAML does not start or clean that process.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: Process lifecycle could be incorrectly encoded as undocumented QaaS YAML.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide documented QaaS lifecycle support or keep startup in an external harness plan.

intent_questions.question: What public docs/schema path proves the capability exists?
intent_questions.self_answer: QaaS public docs/schema support HTTP Transactions, FromFileSystem data-source payload bytes, HttpStatus assertions, and custom assertions by short type name; the ExactHttpTextBody hook is authored but not schema/template/live validated.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may invent a built-in text body assertion or treat sidecar hook code as executable validation.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide template/build/live validation for the custom assertion packet.

intent_questions.question: What inputs, outputs, and side effects prove success?
intent_questions.self_answer: Input is a GET request to /. Outputs are HTTP success and body Hello, world!. Side effects should be read-only for this GET.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may over-assert runtime internals, filesystem changes, permissions, or unrelated endpoints.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide exact public side-effect evidence for any additional assertion.

intent_questions.question: Which negative, malformed, outage, retry, cleanup, and observability cases matter?
intent_questions.self_answer: Negative, malformed, retry, and observability cases remain blocked; this candidate covers only one README-backed happy path plus external cleanup planning.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may claim broad Deno runtime or permission coverage from one README server example.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add exact public negative-case and observability contracts before expanding assertions.

intent_questions.question: Which dependencies must exist: broker, HTTP endpoint, database, Redis, S3, Kubernetes, filesystem, credentials, ports?
intent_questions.self_answer: A managed Deno release binary must be downloaded from the official release asset, hash-verified, launched by absolute path, and given a run-local DENO_DIR; port 8000 must be free.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may trust an ambient deno.exe, mutate user PATH, or miss port cleanup requirements.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Attach managed toolchain and lifecycle transcripts.

intent_questions.question: What can run now, and what must be deferred?
intent_questions.self_answer: Static candidate validation and managed process lifecycle validation can run now. Custom assertion build/schema/template validation, live QaaS act/assert execution, cleanup promotion evidence, and live weak-model validation remain deferred until their evidence exists.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may confuse a candidate packet or lifecycle-only proof with an executable QaaS test.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Attach passing lifecycle, custom assertion build/schema/template, live, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The README server.ts example is the only public behavior contract promoted by this candidate.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: It contains exact server code, command, URL, and response body markers harvested from an immutable Git blob.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may be mistaken for broad Deno runtime coverage.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Harvest exact public evidence for additional behavior before adding assertions.

intent_assumptions.assumption: HTTP 200 is a live lifecycle/QaaS observation, while the exact body comes from README evidence.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The README does not literally state status 200, so status success must be proven by execution.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may overstate source-derived evidence.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Attach public Deno/Fetch docs or keep status as live evidence.

cases.scenario: Call README-backed Deno GET / and assert HTTP success while the sidecar exact text-body assertion remains build/schema/template/live blocked.
setup: Use the managed Deno binary and generated README server.ts.
Use get-root.bin as the byte payload driver for the GET transaction.
action: Run QaaS Runner against test.qaas.yaml after template validation is available.
assertions: HttpStatus StatusCode 200 for GetRoot after live execution.
Exact body Hello, world! is preserved as expected-response evidence but blocked from YAML until the custom assertion is validated.
cleanup: Terminate the tracked Deno process tree.
Remove candidate session-data after live validation.
cases.blocked_reason: Managed toolchain, lifecycle, QaaS template/live, exact plain-text body assertion build/schema/template validation, cleanup, and live weak-model validation have not passed.

assertions: Candidate Runner YAML uses schema-derived HttpStatus StatusCode/OutputNames.
Candidate preserves README-backed plain-text body evidence and includes a sidecar ExactHttpTextBody custom assertion packet that is not wired into active YAML before validation.
Candidate remains blocked until managed toolchain, lifecycle, template, live QaaS, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence pass.

configuration_contract.OutputName: Required transaction output name to inspect.
configuration_contract.ExpectedText: Required exact expected response body text.
configuration_contract.EncodingName: Optional encoding name; defaults to utf-8.

builtins_considered.name: HttpStatus
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: Status-only assertion; does not compare response body bytes.

builtins_considered.name: OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: JSON/CSV field mapping assertion; not exact raw/plain-text body equality.

builtins_considered.name: OutputDeserializableTo
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: Checks deserialization success, not equality with exact public README text.

builtins_considered.name: ObjectOutputJsonSchema
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: Checks JSON schema compatibility, not exact raw/plain-text body equality.

dependency_gates.blocked_reason: Live weak-model validation has not passed.

required_evidence: Public API, CLI, or runtime contract
Public README server.ts command and exact expected body
Managed Deno release asset download, hash, and version transcript
Documented QaaS body assertion or custom hook for plain text
C# build result when code artifacts exist
Live QaaS run/act/assert result when dependency gates are ready

cleanup: No live cleanup was performed during static candidate generation.
validation_sequence: Validate selected immutable public contract evidence.
Validate managed Deno toolchain source and SHA.
Build and schema/template-validate the sidecar ExactHttpTextBody custom assertion before executable promotion.
Run QaaS template validation when a Runner host is available.
Run live QaaS act/assert against tracked Deno process.
Run live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review before promotion.

source_only_blockers.description: The selected evidence only supports the README HTTP hello-world behavior, not broad Deno CLI, Rust runtime, schema, permission, or CI behavior.
required_evidence: Exact public contracts for each additional behavior
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Do not add broad Deno assertions until deterministic public evidence is harvested.

source_only_blockers.description: Live weak-model validation has not passed for this candidate packet.
required_evidence: Airgapped transcript with dry_run false and expected output contract preserved
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Run D:\QaaS\_tools\weak-model-session.ps1 -Airgapped -All -ReasoningEffort none when quota/routing is available.

blocked_reason: Selected Deno candidate passed managed lifecycle, custom ExactHttpTextBody build/template/live validation, and QaaS template/live validation, but executable promotion remains blocked until live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review pass.

validation_advisories.description: The quick-start example uses stale HttpStatus field names, while generated schema docs require StatusCode and OutputNames.
validation_advisories.resolved_by: schema-derived StatusCode/OutputNames template and live validation
